meta.business_type: Agency — Web Design & Development (US market, remote/no storefront)

top_findings: robots.txt still returns 'Disallow: /' for all crawlers — unresolved 5 days after being flagged Critical; every other fix on this list has zero real-world effect until this ships
Canonical tags, sitemap URLs, and OG URLs all point at the non-www apex domain, which itself 308-redirects to www — a self-defeating canonical signal
Site-wide AggregateRating/Review schema (5.0★, 3 reviews) is self-authored and unverifiable — a real Google rich-result policy risk
JSON-LD sameAs links to LinkedIn and Twitter/X both resolve to HTTP 404 — broken entity signals, worse than having none
Sitemap is missing all 6 live blog post pages, and the +91 Indian phone number sitewide contradicts the site's US-market positioning
quick_wins: Change src/app/robots.ts from disallow:"/" to allow:"/" and redeploy — 15 minutes, est. +35-40pt real-world visibility impact
Change SITE.rootDomain in src/lib/site-config.ts to 'www.pinexadigital.com' — fixes canonical/sitemap/OG host mismatch everywhere in one edit
Remove the duplicate '| PinexaDigital' suffix from services/seo and services/maintenance page titles
Add the 6 blog posts to src/app/sitemap.ts by importing from blog-data.ts instead of a hardcoded array
Fix or remove the broken sameAs LinkedIn/Twitter links in layout.tsx JSON-LD

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Sitewide meta robots correctly set to index,follow (fixed from prior audit's global noindex bug)
Canonical tags present on all 11 sitemap pages plus /privacy, /terms, and all 6 blog posts (host is wrong, but the tag itself is consistently implemented)
HSTS preload-eligible, plus X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy now ship on every route (new since prior audit)
All pages confirmed server-rendered (is_spa: False), zero console errors
6 blog post detail pages now return 200 (previously all 404) and /api/contact now exists

findings.title: robots.txt still disallows the entire site — unresolved regression (5 days)
findings.description: src/app/robots.ts hardcodes rules:{userAgent:"*",disallow:"/"}. Confirmed live via curl. Blocks Googlebot, Bingbot, and every AI crawler from every URL, nullifying all other fixes shipped this cycle.
findings.recommendation: Change to allow:"/" (or remove the rules override), redeploy, verify with curl, then request re-crawl in GSC/Bing Webmaster Tools.

findings.title: Canonical/sitemap/OG URLs use the wrong host (apex instead of www)
findings.description: SITE.rootDomain in site-config.ts is 'pinexadigital.com' (no www), but the live site serves at www.pinexadigital.com (apex 308-redirects). Every canonical tag points at a URL that itself redirects.
findings.recommendation: Change rootDomain to 'www.pinexadigital.com' in src/lib/site-config.ts; regenerate sitemap and redeploy.

findings.title: Duplicate brand name in two <title> tags
findings.description: services/seo/page.tsx and services/maintenance/page.tsx hardcode '| PinexaDigital' inside their own title, and the layout template appends it again, producing '...| PinexaDigital | PinexaDigital' live.
findings.recommendation: Remove the manual '| PinexaDigital' suffix from both files; let the layout title template add it once.

findings.title: No Content-Security-Policy header
findings.description: 4 of 5 standard security headers ship via next.config.ts; CSP is the one missing, leaving XSS/injection mitigation weaker than it should be.
findings.recommendation: Add a CSP header, starting permissive (allowing 'unsafe-inline' for Next.js hydration scripts) and tightening iteratively.

findings.title: Sitemap.xml omits all 6 live blog post pages
findings.description: sitemap.ts uses a hardcoded 11-URL array and never imports posts from blog-data.ts, so all 6 published, 200-returning blog posts are absent from the sitemap.
findings.recommendation: Generate blog post sitemap entries dynamically from the same data source that powers /blog and blog/[slug].

findings.title: Sitemap lastModified uses request-time new Date(), not real content dates
findings.description: All 11 sitemap URLs share an identical, fabricated lastmod timestamp generated at request time, which Google discounts as an inaccurate freshness signal.
findings.recommendation: Use real per-page last-modified dates (post 'date' field for blog, static constants for marketing pages).

findings.title: og-image.jpg still returns 404
findings.description: metadataBase + openGraph.images + twitter.images all reference /og-image.jpg, which 404s — social shares render with no preview image.
findings.recommendation: Add a 1200x630 branded JPEG at public/og-image.jpg.

findings.title: No IndexNow implementation
findings.description: No IndexNow key file or API integration found. Optional, but would help re-establish visibility quickly once robots.txt is fixed.
findings.recommendation: Generate an IndexNow key, host it at /{key}.txt, call the IndexNow API on deploy.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Blog posts are real and substantive (900+ words sampled), dated, with BlogPosting/BreadcrumbList schema — prior 404 issue fully resolved
Transparent, specific pricing ($997/$2,497/Custom) with FAQ block — rare trust signal for this vertical
Service sub-pages are well-differentiated (700-1,000 words each, not template-duplicated) with numbered process steps
Consistent metadata (title/description/OG/canonical) present on every page checked

findings.title: Site-wide testimonials/reviews are unverifiable and schema-embedded as fact
findings.description: 3 homepage testimonials (first name + last initial only, no company, no photo, no link) are simultaneously asserted as structured Review/AggregateRating data (5.0★, 3 reviews) with no third-party verification path.
findings.recommendation: Replace with real, attributable reviews (full name + company + link, or sourced from a verified platform) or drop the Review/AggregateRating schema until real reviews exist.

findings.title: Portfolio page has essentially no actual project imagery
findings.description: parse_html.py found exactly one on /portfolio — the site logo. The page whose entire purpose is visual proof-of-work ships with no screenshots of actual client sites.
findings.recommendation: Add real screenshots (or clearly-labeled mockups) for each portfolio entry.

findings.title: Zero named human authorship anywhere on the site
findings.description: Blog posts use Organization-only authorship (no Person, no bio, no headshot) on all 6 posts. /about describes 'a focused team' with no named individuals.
findings.recommendation: Add at least one named author with bio/credentials to /about and attribute blog posts to that person.

findings.title: US-targeting site displays an Indian phone number sitewide
findings.description: +91 78198 32001 appears as a live tel: link in the footer of every page while all copy and schema explicitly target US businesses/customers.
findings.recommendation: Provision a US-based number (Google Voice, VoIP, or toll-free) for the public-facing footer/contact page.

findings.title: /pricing is thin relative to purchase-decision weight
findings.description: 250 words for a page that has to carry an entire buying decision — no case studies, no process narrative, no risk-reversal language beyond tier cards + FAQ.
findings.recommendation: Add a short 'what happens after you pay' narrative and at least one linked case study per tier.

findings.title: No dedicated author/team page or bios
findings.description: No /team or /about#team section with individual bios, which would reinforce both Expertise and Experience E-E-A-T pillars.
findings.recommendation: Add a 3-4 person 'who we are' grid on /about with real names/roles/photos.

findings.title: Blog post dates are not chronological
findings.description: Post dates span a wide, non-chronological range, reading as backdated/batch-published rather than an ongoing cadence.
findings.recommendation: Verify and correct publish dates; add an 'updated' date for refreshed evergreen posts.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Title/meta/heading hygiene is clean on 9 of 11 pages
Breadcrumb-based internal linking is consistent site-wide
3 of 4 SERP-backwards-analyzed keywords show ALIGNED-to-MEDIUM page-type match against what Google currently rewards

findings.title: No comparison/differentiation content anywhere on the site
findings.description: All 4 keywords analyzed via SERP-backwards SXO review show a directory/listicle competitor ('Top 10 agencies') in 40-70% of results, but the site has zero comparison or 'why us' content.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Why PinexaDigital vs. a typical agency' comparison block to the homepage and /services/web-design.

findings.title: No SEO retainer pricing shown anywhere
findings.description: /services/seo and /pricing cover only one-time web-design packages. Competing 'SEO agency for small business' results anchor at explicit monthly prices (e.g. $499/mo).
findings.recommendation: Add an SEO/Growth monthly retainer tier to /pricing and link it from the /services/seo CTA.

findings.title: Sitemap architecture issues (non-canonical host, missing blog URLs)
findings.description: See Technical SEO findings — sitemap lists non-www URLs that redirect on every entry and omits all 6 blog posts.
findings.recommendation: Fix rootDomain and dynamically include blog posts in sitemap.ts.

findings.title: FAQ content lacks FAQPage schema on 3 pages
findings.description: /services/web-design, /services/seo, and /pricing all have visible, well-structured Q&A accordions with no FAQPage JSON-LD wrapping them.
findings.recommendation: Add FAQPage schema matching the existing visible accordion content (no SERP rich-result benefit post-May-2026 policy change, but valuable for AI/GEO citation).

findings.title: Pricing page has no trust signals at the point of highest purchase anxiety
findings.description: /pricing shows 3 tiered cards and an FAQ but zero testimonials, guarantees, or client logos next to the price table.
findings.recommendation: Insert a condensed 'Trusted by 50+ businesses / 5.0 avg rating' strip directly above or beside the pricing cards.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: WebSite + ProfessionalService graph, BreadcrumbList site-wide, per-service Service schema, ItemList/Offer pricing, and Blog/BlogPosting are all now correctly implemented and confirmed server-rendered — up from 18/100 on 2026-06-29
All JSON-LD validated: correct @context, valid types, absolute URLs, ISO 8601 dates, no placeholder text

findings.title: Site-wide AggregateRating/Review is a self-serving, unverifiable review policy risk
findings.description: The ProfessionalService node carries aggregateRating (5.0/3 reviews) and 3 embedded Review objects with no third-party verification path and no datePublished — a real Google rich-result policy risk (self-serving reviews).
findings.recommendation: Remove Review/AggregateRating schema until real, verifiable third-party reviews exist, or source them from Google Business Profile/Clutch and link the profile.

findings.title: ProfessionalService vs Organization — type appropriateness
findings.description: ProfessionalService is a LocalBusiness subtype implying a physical/service-area presence, but this is a remote-only agency with no address in the markup.
findings.recommendation: Switch to Organization type, which supports all currently-used properties without implying a local-business presence.

findings.title: Blog posts missing image on BlogPosting
findings.description: Post type in blog-data.ts has no image field, so BlogPosting JSON-LD can't populate one — needed for Article rich results.
findings.recommendation: Add a 1200x630 image field to each post and include it in the JSON-LD.

categories.name: Performance (Core Web Vitals)
what_works: CLS measured at 0 across all pages/profiles — next/image consistently emits explicit width/height
INP proxy (TBT) is Good — no long main-thread tasks, no third-party analytics scripts found
Vercel edge caching functioning (X-Vercel-Cache: HIT), TTFB in 180-310ms lab range
Fonts preloaded via next/font/google with display:swap — no FOIT/CLS risk

findings.title: Mobile LCP is borderline at the Good threshold
findings.description: Simulated mobile LCP measured 2.7s, right at the 2.5s cutoff; desktop LCP is excellent (1.1s). Lab-only estimate, no CrUX field data available.
findings.recommendation: Add fetchpriority=high on the hero/LCP image, avoid loading=lazy on it, confirm appropriately-capped resolution for mobile.

findings.title: ~48 KiB of estimated unused JavaScript
findings.description: Lighthouse flagged unused JS in first-party chunks, a minor contributor to mobile TBT.
findings.recommendation: Audit chunk composition for unused code paths; confirm route-level code-splitting.

findings.title: No CrUX/PageSpeed API key configured
findings.description: All figures are single-run lab simulations, not real Chrome UX Report field data.
findings.recommendation: Configure a PageSpeed Insights API key once traffic accumulates for authoritative field-data validation.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: llms.txt now exists and is well-formed (new since prior audit)
/services, /pricing, /about substantially expanded with genuine FAQ-style Q&A and self-contained direct-answer passages
All pages are server-rendered (is_spa: False) — technical crawlability is not the bottleneck once robots.txt is fixed
Blog post opening passages are legitimately extractable AI-citation content

findings.title: robots.txt still blocks every AI crawler — unresolved
findings.description: GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, OAI-SearchBot, and Google-Extended are all blocked by the same Disallow:/ rule as regular search engines. Nothing else in this category matters until this is fixed.
findings.recommendation: Same fix as Technical SEO #1: allow crawling. Estimated impact: +35-40 pts to this category alone.

findings.title: sameAs entity links point to non-existent profiles (404s)
findings.description: Both LinkedIn and Twitter/X sameAs URLs in the ProfessionalService schema return HTTP 404 and aren't linked anywhere in visible HTML — a false structured-data claim, worse than no signal at all.
findings.recommendation: Create and verify both profiles before relying on them, or remove sameAs entirely until real profiles exist.

findings.title: Headline stats remain unsourced and undated
findings.description: +127% growth, 50+ businesses, 5.0 stars, 40% traffic lift, 98% satisfaction all lack methodology, date range, or verifiable link.
findings.recommendation: Add one sentence of provenance per stat, or link the rating claim to an external, checkable review platform.

findings.title: No FAQPage schema despite existing FAQ content
findings.description: Genuine Q&A content exists on /services and /pricing but is not wrapped in FAQPage JSON-LD anywhere.
findings.recommendation: Add FAQPage schema mirroring the existing on-page Q&A; low effort since content already exists.

findings.title: +91 phone number contradicts areaServed: United States
findings.description: Same contact-geography mismatch as flagged in Content Quality, relevant here for AI entity-resolution trust scoring.
findings.recommendation: Provision a US virtual number and update the schema telephone field.

categories.name: Images
what_works: next/image serves WebP/AVIF automatically with responsive srcSet and explicit width/height on every image found — 0 CLS risk measured across all pages/viewports
Lazy-loading confirmed on below-fold images

findings.title: Portfolio page has essentially no real project images (cross-ref Content Quality)
findings.description: Only 1 image (the logo) renders on /portfolio despite it being the site's dedicated visual-proof page.
findings.recommendation: Add real screenshots for each portfolio entry — see Content Quality Critical #2.

findings.title: Homepage relies on generic stock photography
findings.description: Unsplash-sourced 'team at work' / 'design process' images rather than real team or project photography — a credibility risk for a trust-dependent purchase.
findings.recommendation: Replace with real team/work photos or niche-specific custom photography.

findings.title: No dedicated alt-text audit performed
findings.description: This pass focused on technical image delivery (format/sizing/CLS) rather than a full alt-text quality review across all images.
findings.recommendation: Run a follow-up alt-text-specific pass once portfolio imagery is added.

additional_categories.name: Sitemap Structure
additional_categories.note: Findings folded into Technical SEO and On-Page SEO above; full detail in findings/sitemap.md

additional_categories.name: Visual / Mobile UX
additional_categories.note: Strong above-the-fold execution; findings/visual.md has full screenshot-based detail

additional_categories.name: Search Experience (SXO)
additional_categories.note: SERP-backwards keyword analysis; findings folded into On-Page SEO above; full detail in findings/sxo.md

additional_categories.name: Backlinks / Authority
additional_categories.note: New site, no measurable backlink profile yet via Common Crawl (Tier 0, no Moz/Bing/DataForSEO configured). This is a baseline, not a defect — excluded from the weighted health score. Full detail in findings/backlinks.md

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Fix src/app/robots.ts: change disallow:"/" to allow:"/" and redeploy — the single highest-priority item on this entire audit
Fix SITE.rootDomain in src/lib/site-config.ts to 'www.pinexadigital.com' to resolve the canonical/sitemap/OG host mismatch
Remove or fix the site-wide AggregateRating/Review schema (self-serving review policy risk)
Fix or remove the broken sameAs LinkedIn/Twitter links
After deploy: verify robots.txt live, submit for re-crawl in Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Add real portfolio screenshots (replace the logo-only placeholder page)
Add named human authorship (founder/team bio + Person schema on blog posts)
Replace the +91 phone number with a US-based number sitewide
Add the 6 blog posts to sitemap.ts dynamically
Add FAQPage schema to /services/web-design, /services/seo, and /pricing
Fix duplicate title tags on services/seo and services/maintenance
Add a comparison/'why us' content block to the homepage and /services/web-design
Add an SEO monthly retainer price to /pricing and /services/seo

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Source and date the homepage headline stats (+127%, 50+, 5.0, 40%, 98%)
Add a trust-signal strip (ratings/testimonials) directly on /pricing
Expand /pricing content depth with a post-purchase process narrative
Add Content-Security-Policy header
Replace generic stock photography with real team/project imagery
Fix mobile nav tap-target sizing (32px → 44px+)
Add fetchpriority=high to the homepage hero/LCP image
Correct non-chronological blog post publish dates
Add a CSP header and IndexNow key file

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Once real traffic accumulates, configure a PageSpeed Insights API key to validate LCP against real CrUX field data
Build off-site authority signals: verified LinkedIn/Twitter profiles, Google Business Profile, Clutch.co listing
Configure Moz or Bing Webmaster API keys to unlock real backlink discovery beyond Common Crawl's quarterly snapshot
Re-run this audit after Phase 1 ships to confirm indexation recovery and re-baseline all category scores
